div.span: i have custom OBS overlays for coding, electronics, and gaming
the first version was worse. that's the point.
if there's a PCB and a problem i'll be happy for weeks
i care about the details nobody will notice
my home lab has more uptime than most startups
the hot tub still works btw
firmware engineer who forgot to stop at the backend
the LED blinks. we ship it.
sometimes i read the datasheet twice
i don't stop because i'm done. i stop because i'm tired.
i spent hours on OBS overlays i never go live with
i have too many open tabs and half-finished PCBs
building an open-source robot arm. currently in pieces.
my OBS setup is production-ready. i don't stream.
C++ in the morning. TypeScript at night.
git blame says i did that. git blame is correct.
i speak microcontroller and server fluently
i write docs because future-me is grateful
yes those OBS scenes have custom animated backgrounds. i made them.
CET/CEST. usually awake.
works on my machine. also works on the server. rare.
i like projects with sharp edges
ships things
the readme is longer than the actual project
if something annoyed me once, i'll fix it so it never does again
it compiles. mostly.
i make things blink. then i make them talk to a server.
it's 2am and the firmware finally deep-sleeps correctly
i write firmware. also the backend. also the frontend.
once saw a problem at an event. now it's a SaaS.
i care a lot about the things i make
i built a dev dashboard because i didn't like my other dev dashboards
i think about edge cases in the shower
got tired of spreadsheets at gaming fairs
started as a gaming fair volunteer. built a product instead.
